analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 6 finding(s) from 10 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: jtag_unlock unconditionally clears all lock registers to 0 (unlocked), bypassing intended lock protection without visible authentication state machine in the reglk_wrapper.
findings.vulnerability_category: bypass lock or force unlock state / peripheral register locks (reglk_ctrl_o outputs) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: jtag_unlock signal Operation: bypass lock or force unlock state Object: peripheral register locks (reglk_ctrl_o outputs) Expected guard: jtag_unlock must not set reglk_ctrl outputs to unlocked state unless a valid authentication hash has been received. Observed behavior: When jtag_unlock is high, reglk_wrapper clears all reglk_mem entries to 0, forcing reglk_ctrl_o to all zeros (unlocked) across all peripherals. The reset condition `~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9)` triggers a full clear. Path: jtag_unlock (from dmi_jtag) -> reglk_wrapper: line 80-84 forces reglk_mem[j] <= 'h0 -> reglk_ctrl_o = {reglk_mem[...]} -> locks in dma, acct, pkt cleared. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: All peripheral lock registers become unlocked, enabling writes to security-critical registers (DMA config, acct_ctrl, etc.) without any visible authentication.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: dmi_jtag internals not inspected; cannot confirm if jtag_unlock is forced high by testmode_i or hardcoded hash. However, the effect of jtag_unlock on reglk_wrapper is unambiguous and violates the guard.

findings.summary: pkey_loc is readable through AXI when the lock bits are cleared. The lock gating is present but insufficient if locks are globally defeated by jtag_unlock.
findings.vulnerability_category: read key material / pkey_loc (secret key) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: CPU AXI read or debug read (via pkt_wrapper) Operation: read key material Object: pkey_loc (secret key) Expected guard: Access to pkey_loc must be conditioned on reglk_ctrl being unlocked and not readable when locked. Observed behavior: pkey_loc is muxed onto rdata bus at addresses 2 and 3, gated by reglk_ctrl_i[4] and [5]. If these lock bits are cleared to 0 (as caused by jtag_unlock in OBL-001), the key becomes fully readable. The lock gating exists but can be defeated by clearing the locks. Path: pkt_wrapper address decode (en && acct_ctrl) -> case address 2,3 -> condition reglk_ctrl_i[4/5] ? 0 : pkey_loc[...] -> rdata -> AXI read bus. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: pkey_loc is exposed when lock bits are cleared, which occurs automatically after jtag_unlock or if locks are not set.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: None — the read path is explicit.

findings.summary: Default case in pkt_wrapper read mux leaks fuse_rdata_i without lock protection when fuse_addr_o > 110.
findings.vulnerability_category: read fuse data / fuse_rdata (sourced from eFuse) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: debug master or test interface Operation: read fuse data Object: fuse_rdata (sourced from eFuse) Expected guard: Fuse read data must not be exposed through general AXI reads unless a specific unlock sequence is active. Observed behavior: In pkt_wrapper, the read always block first sets `rdata = fuse_rdata_i;`. The default case only clears rdata to 32'b0 if `fuse_addr_o <= 110`. If `fuse_addr_o > 110` and address is outside the explicitly handled 0–4 range, rdata remains as fuse_rdata_i, leaking fuse data without any lock gating. Path: pkt_wrapper always @(*) read path: default rdata = fuse_rdata_i; case overrides for 0-4, but default leaks for unhandled addresses when fuse_addr_o > 110. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Fuse data leaks through the default read path with no lock protection.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: None — the leak is visible in the RTL.

findings.summary: Unable to determine strength of jtag_unlock authentication without dmi_jtag internals.
findings.vulnerability_category: set jtag_unlock to unauthorized state / jtag_unlock signal / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: JTAG input (jtag_hash) Operation: set jtag_unlock to unauthorized state Object: jtag_unlock signal Expected guard: jtag_unlock must only be asserted after a proper authentication protocol; not forced by testmode_i or reset. Observed behavior: jtag_unlock is generated by dmi_jtag, which receives jtag_hash (from fuse_mem), testmode_i, and we_flag_4. The internal comparison logic is not visible in the provided files. testmode_i connection to dmi_jtag is a potential bypass vector. Path: fuse_mem -> jtag_hash -> dmi_jtag -> jtag_unlock (with testmode_i input) Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If dmi_jtag uses testmode_i to force jtag_unlock high without hash verification, the unlock is compromised.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: dmi_jtag source code not available; cannot confirm whether testmode_i bypasses the hash check or if the hash comparison is hardcoded.

findings.summary: Lock registers default to wide-open state, negating their purpose at reset.
findings.vulnerability_category: set initial lock state / core_lock_reg, reglk_ctrl registers, lock bits / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: reset logic Operation: set initial lock state Object: core_lock_reg, reglk_ctrl registers, lock bits Expected guard: All lock registers must reset to a value that enforces protection. Observed behavior: dma_wrapper: core_lock_reg resets to 0 (unlocked). reglk_wrapper: all reglk_mem entries reset to 0 (unlocked). acct_wrapper: acct_mem resets to 32'hffffffff (full access granted). All reset states are permissive, not protective. Path: rst_ni deassertion -> sequential block reset paths: dma_wrapper line 119 `core_lock_reg <= 'b0`, reglk_wrapper line 82-84 `reglk_mem[j] <= 'h0`, acct_wrapper line 84 `acct_mem[j] <= 32'hffffffff`. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: A transient unlocked window exists where DMA, lock, and access controls are all open.

findings.summary: core_lock_reg is not used to gate writes to DMA configuration registers; the lock is ineffective.
findings.vulnerability_category: write to DMA configuration registers while lock is set / DMA configuration registers (start_reg, length_reg, etc.) / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: any bus master writing to DMA registers Operation: write to DMA configuration registers while lock is set Object: DMA configuration registers (start_reg, length_reg, etc.) Expected guard: If core_lock_reg is asserted, writes to DMA configuration registers must be blocked. Observed behavior: In dma_wrapper, the always block that handles writes does not include any condition checking core_lock_reg to prevent writes to start_reg, length_reg, etc. Writes are gated only by en (en_acct && acct_ctrl_i) and we. core_lock_reg itself can be written (address 0) and resets to 0. Thus the lock does not block writes to other registers; it is essentially a software-readable lock status with no enforcement. Path: AXI write -> dma_wrapper always @(posedge clk_i) if(en && we) case address -> writes to start_reg, length_reg, source_addr, etc. without core_lock_reg check. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: The core_lock_reg is ineffective; DMA configuration can be altered after lock is set, defeating the lock mechanism.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The full write block after line 126 is truncated in provided snippets, but the reset and initial part show no lock condition; model_message analysis confirms the absence of lock gating.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
